subject: [PATCH] tracing: Fix context switch counter truncation

reviews.author: Masami Hiramatsu (Google)
reviews.summary: Identified the patch as correct and provided an Acked-by tag, indicating that it can be applied to the kernel.

2026-07-17.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the tracing system where context switch counter truncation occurs due to an unsigned int being assigned to a u64 variable, causing lost bits and incorrect reporting of changes.

reviews.author: Masami Hiramatsu (Google)
reviews.summary: Identified the patch as a fix for the context switch counter truncation issue and provided an ACK with Acked-by.

2026-07-18.patch_summary: The patch fixes a context switch counter truncation issue in the tracing subsystem, where the sampled count is stored as an unsigned long long to preserve its full value.
